package.json.role: for a Vite + React + TypeScript app targeting a Windows kiosk UI.

vite.config.ts.role: exporting default defineConfig({ plugins:[react()], resolve:{ alias:{ '@': fileURLToPath(new URL('./src', import.meta.url)) } }, server:{ port:5173 } }).

tsconfig.json.role: for Vite React TS using project references pattern.

index.html.role:
Create root index.html with , html lang en, head meta charset utf-8, meta viewport, title DusGuru Kiosk, body containing
, script type module src=/src/main.tsx.

src/styles/index.css.role: with Tailwind directives @tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;.

src/utils/env.ts.role: exporting function getApiBaseUrl(): string reading import.meta.env.VITE_API_BASE_URL as string | undefined.

src/components/layout/ScreenContainer.ts.role: exporting const ScreenContainer: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<{ className?: string }>> applying classes: min-h-screen w-full bg-black text-white flex flex-col.

src/app/providers/AppProviders.tsx.role: exporting const AppProviders wrapping children with QueryClientProvider using a module-level const queryClient = new QueryClient({ defaultOptions:{ queries:{ staleTime: 60_000, retry: 1 } } }).

.gitignore.role: Create .gitignore ignoring node_modules, dist, .vite, .env, .env.local, .DS_Store, npm-debug.log*, yarn-debug.log*, yarn-error.log*, pnpm-debug.log*, coverage, .idea, .vscode (keep repo clean for...

README.md.role: documenting Milestone 0: install deps with npm install, copy .env.example to .env, run npm run dev.

src/models/types.ts.role: exporting TypeScript types/interfaces for the API domain: AppData, Button, Trailer, GalleryItem.

src/services/api.ts.role: exporting an Axios instance apiClient configured with baseURL from getApiBaseUrl(), timeout 10000.

src/services/normalization.ts.role: exporting function getAppData(response: unknown, targetButtonId: string): AppData.

src/components/transition/FadeTransition.role: Export default function FadeTransition(props:{ screenKey: string; children: React.ReactNode; className?: string }).
